Risks: Agent bypass: agents will develop workarounds if the portal creates more work or fails to handle exceptions well (as is the current practice); named as a high risk by Gareth
Mitigations: Workflow mapping; full training and concise agent-user documentation to be provided
Flags: Backlog items: agent user-acceptance testing (UAT); agent training; reporting requirement: weekly feedback on portal quality, usage and workarounds to track workarounds

Risks: Customer drop-off: customers attempt portal, have a bad experience, bypass portal and return to old methods - consider that customers have no documented digital ability, awareness of need for change, desire for change
Mitigations: Testing with users; clear error message; clear "Speak-to-Agent" (S2A) option; feedback opportunities
Flags: Backlog item: UX testing; reporting requirement: portal abandonment rate and S2A selection rate

Risks: Inconsistent reinforcement: across all stakeholder groups, there is no clear process to enforce portal use or adoption; risk of falling into old habits
Mitigations: Adoption KPIs for agents, managers, executives
Flags: Reporting requirement: portal adoption / use rate (perhaps a measure of cases attempted and solved)

Risks: Executive disengagement: over time, portal value goes unassessed and lose clarity on portal operational benefits
Mitigations: Monthly reporting of adoption statistics, case handling success and agent/manager feedback sent to executives
Flags: Reporting requirement: portal adoption / use rate (perhaps a measure of cases attempted and solved); executive briefing message: regular reporting to validate portal and identify improvements

Risks: Agent knowledge gaps: at portal handover (S2A) or data retrieval
Mitigations: Define and document clear escalation protocols in guide for agents and managers; manager- and agent-user documentation for DB hybrid history dashboard
Flags: Backlog items: documentation for escalation protocols and (agent-facing) customer history dashboard; agent training
